breaking_news.title: TeamPCP Compromises Trivy and LiteLLM in Massive Supply Chain Attack
breaking_news.summary:
The TeamPCP threat actor has executed a widespread supply chain attack compromising critical security tools including Trivy, Checkmarx, and the LiteLLM Python package. Malicious versions of LiteLLM were published on PyPI containing credential harvesters and Kubernetes lateral movement toolkits before being removed.

Over 1,000 enterprise SaaS environments are currently impacted, with the campaign escalating to include collaboration with the Lapsus$ extortion group. This incident highlights severe risks in CI/CD pipelines and open-source dependencies, forcing developers to rotate credentials immediately.

breaking_news.title: DarkSword iOS Exploit Leaked Threatens 270 Million Devices
breaking_news.summary:
A critical iOS exploit chain known as DarkSword has been leaked online, putting up to 270 million iPhones at risk of unauthorized access. CISA has ordered U.S. government agencies to patch three specific vulnerabilities targeted in cryptocurrency theft and cyberespionage attacks.

The exploit kit allows attackers to bypass security controls and access sensitive data on unpatched devices without user interaction. Security researchers warn that the widespread availability of this tool significantly increases the threat landscape for mobile users and enterprise BYOD policies.

breaking_news.title: Hackers Actively Exploit Quest KACE SMA Zero-Day Flaw
breaking_news.summary:
Threat actors are actively exploiting a maximum-severity security flaw (CVE-2025-32975) in Quest KACE Systems Management Appliance systems exposed to the internet. Arctic Wolf observed malicious activity consistent with this exploitation starting the week of March 9, 2026.

The vulnerability allows for complete system compromise, enabling attackers to gain control over enterprise management infrastructure. Organizations using Quest KACE SMA are urged to apply emergency patches immediately to prevent unauthorized access and data theft.

breaking_news.title: Oracle Releases Emergency Patch for Critical Identity Manager RCE
breaking_news.summary:
Oracle has issued an emergency security update to address a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-21992) in Identity Manager and Web Services Manager. This flaw enables unauthenticated remote code execution over HTTP, posing a severe risk of full system compromise.

The vulnerability carries a CVSS score of 9.8 and may have already been exploited in the wild by threat actors seeking access to identity management systems. Customers are strongly advised to apply the provided updates or mitigations as soon as possible to secure their infrastructure.

breaking_news.title: Citrix Urges Patching of Critical NetScaler Data Leak Flaws
breaking_news.summary:
Citrix has patched two vulnerabilities in NetScaler ADC and Gateway, including a critical flaw similar to the previously exploited CitrixBleed issues. The primary vulnerability (CVE-2026-3055) allows unauthenticated attackers to leak sensitive data from the appliance's memory.

This flaw is classified as an out-of-bounds read with a CVSS score of 9.3 and requires immediate attention for systems configured as a SAML IDP. Security firms warn that the vulnerability is poised for exploitation, urging administrators to update their appliances without delay.

breaking_news.title: Stryker Medical Devices Wiped in Alleged Iranian Cyberattack
breaking_news.summary:
Medical device manufacturer Stryker confirmed that alleged Iranian cyber actors wiped more than 200,000 company devices during a recent cyberattack. The firm is now ramping production lines back up two weeks after the incident disrupted operations.

The FBI has published an alert describing the specific malware used by the Iranian government hackers in this campaign. This attack underscores the growing threat to critical healthcare infrastructure and the potential for state-sponsored actors to target medical supply chains.

breaking_news.title: Lapsus$ Claims Breach of Pharma Giant AstraZeneca
breaking_news.summary:
The cybercrime group Lapsus$ has claimed to hack pharmaceutical giant AstraZeneca, stealing approximately 3GB of sensitive data. The alleged leak includes credentials, internal code repositories, and employee information, though the company has not yet confirmed the breach.

If confirmed, this incident could represent one of the most serious healthcare cyber incidents of the year, potentially aiding attackers in mapping systems and launching phishing attacks. The group advertised the breach on a dark web forum associated with their extortion activities.

breaking_news.title: TP-Link Warns of Critical Router Auth Bypass Flaw
breaking_news.summary:
TP-Link has patched a high-severity flaw in Archer NX routers that allows attackers to bypass authentication and install malicious firmware. The vulnerability impacts multiple models and enables privileged HTTP actions without authentication, creating a high risk of compromise.

Users are urged to apply security updates immediately to prevent unauthorized firmware uploads and configuration operations. This flaw highlights the ongoing security risks in consumer IoT devices and the importance of timely patching to maintain network integrity.

breaking_news.title: Hackers Expose Millions of Crime Tipster Records
breaking_news.summary:
A hacker group known as Internet Yiff Machine claims to have compromised systems used by Crime Stoppers programs worldwide via P3 Global Intel. The group supplied a cache of more than 8.3 million records spanning from 1987 to 2025 to Straight Arrow News.

The data reportedly includes crime tips submitted through law enforcement agencies, schools, and parts of the US federal government. This exposure poses significant risks to the safety and privacy of individuals who reported crimes, potentially endangering witnesses and victims.

breaking_news.title: FBI and CISA Warn of Russian Phishing Targeting Messaging Apps
breaking_news.summary:
The FBI and CISA have issued a public service announcement warning about ongoing Russian-linked phishing campaigns targeting Signal and WhatsApp accounts. The goal is to hijack accounts belonging to senior officials, military personnel, and journalists without breaking encryption.

Threat actors pose as support staff to trick users into granting access, allowing them to steal messages and contacts. This campaign highlights the evolving tactics of state-sponsored actors to compromise secure communication channels through social engineering.
